title: Microsoft prepares digitization of physical Xbox games and a console without a disc drive
summary: The company tests a system that allows converting Xbox One and Series discs into digital licenses, while Project Helix would arrive without an optical drive.

title: Sony announces the end of physical game discs for PlayStation
summary: The company will stop selling discs starting in 2028, removes the disc drive in PS6, and forces purchase of digital licenses.

title: Intel increases price of its Core Ultra 7 270K and 250K CPUs by up to $50
summary: The manufacturer raised the suggested price of its flagship desktop processors, according to multiple reports.

title: OpenAI offers to donate 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
summary: The AI company proposes giving an equity stake to the US government in exchange for regulatory support.

title: SpaceX shows AI smartphone prototype while Elon Musk denies it
summary: Despite reports that SpaceX presented a thin phone with xAI technology to investors, Musk called it categorically false.

title: Spain orders ban on Palantir over national security risk
summary: The Spanish government considers Palantir a threat while NATO, a partner of Spain, has put it in charge of its security.

title: Anthropic negotiates with Samsung to manufacture a custom AI chip
summary: The AI company explores creating its own processor with Samsung, although it assures that Nvidia remains relevant.

title: Noctua prepares low-profile coolers for AMD and new Seasonic power supplies
summary: Noctua's roadmap includes a low-profile cooler for AM5 and collaborations with Seasonic on power supplies.

title: Prototype of Radeon RX 7900 XTX appears with red PCB and only 16 GB memory
summary: An engineering sample of AMD's flagship GPU shows reduced specifications and a design without a backplate.

title: Apple plans to launch new iPad Pro and MacBook Pro in early 2027
summary: The company prepares major updates for its professional devices, including the M7 chip.

title: Anthropic reduces Claude Code prompt by 80% due to demand from Fable 5 models
summary: Fable 5's new AI models prefer more concise instructions, leading to a cut in Claude's system prompt.

title: EU confirms record fine of €4.1 billion to Google for abuse of dominant position
summary: The Court of Justice of the European Union upholds the highest antitrust penalty imposed on the company.

title: US government suffers new massive cyberattack
summary: Authorities confirm a security breach in federal systems, without yet revealing the full extent.

title: Meta plans to rent AI computing capacity and will compete directly with AWS
summary: Mark Zuckerberg's company would offer AI infrastructure to third parties, causing stock market falls.

title: Nvidia funds AI startups to reduce dependence on large chip suppliers
summary: The company invests in startups to diversify the ecosystem and counteract the power of Big Tech.

title: Microsoft launches a company dedicated to AI deployment with $2.5 billion
summary: The tech giant creates a new subsidiary to accelerate the adoption of artificial intelligence in businesses.

title: AI agents already perform 16% of freelance jobs with professional quality
summary: In just eight months, the capability of autonomous assistants has gone from 2.5% to 16% in skilled tasks.

title: Japan's Supreme Court rules that AI cannot be listed as inventor in patents
summary: The Japanese court establishes that only humans can be recognized as inventors, setting a legal precedent.

title: BYD to install 1,500 kW ultra-fast chargers in Spain by 2027
summary: The Chinese manufacturer copies Tesla's model and plans a network of high-power charging stations.

title: Singapore seizes $42 million mansion from alleged Nvidia GPU smugglers
summary: Authorities freeze accounts and assets of individuals accused of illegally exporting AI servers to China.
